title: Mapper

{{Quote|Then leave it to Mapper. He's a merciless killer. He'll not only eliminate her, he'll dispose of her in such a way that they'll never find the body. He's very fond of construction sites and duracrete foundations.|[[Joram Kithe]], lying to [[Cherek Tuhm]] and [[Livintius Sazet]]|<ref name="League of Spies">{{InsiderCite|73|The League of Spies}}</ref>}}
'''Mapper''' was the name of an [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|Advanced Recon Commando]] who served the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Mapper was one of the ARC [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] assigned to the [[Battle of Pengalan IV|attack]] on [[Pengalan IV]], which turned out to be a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] trap. Mapper's [[Limb/Legends|leg]] was badly injured when the [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|LAAT/i]] [[Gunship/Legends|gunship]] he was in was shot down. Although it was suggested that Mapper be [[Death/Legends|put down]] due to his injury, the [[Accountant/Legends|accountant]] [[Joram Kithe]], who had been sent to evaluate the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]]' performance, came up with a plan that allowed the unit to complete the mission while taking Mapper with them.

While recovering from his injury, Mapper was served as the partner and bodyguard of Kithe, who had now accepted a commission as a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] in [[Republic Intelligence/Legends|Republic Intelligence]]. Following his recovery, Mapper and Kithe were assigned a mission to the planet [[Tarhassan]] where they were tasked to a team of agents assigned with rescuing [[Edbit Teeks]]. Kithe and Mapper were able to successfully complete their assigned mission with help from [[Tinian Hanther]], the most competent member of the group to which they were attached.

==Biography==
===Pengalan IV===
{{Quote|By the way, your nickname is now Mapper. Don't forget it.|[[Joram Kithe]] to Mapper|<ref name="The Pengalan Tradeoff" />}}
Mapper was an [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|ARC]] trooper who served the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="The Pengalan Tradeoff" /> Like other [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]], he was a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] of the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]].<ref name="Databank" /> Mapper was assigned to a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Battle of Pengalan IV|attack]] on [[Pengalan IV]]. The [[Accountant/Legends|accountant]] [[Joram Kithe]] was assigned to evaluate the clone [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]]' performance, unaware that the [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] in the unit were all ARCs. However, the attack turned into a rout when the factory the Republic was targeting turned out to be a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] trap. The ARCs' [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|LAAT/i]] [[Gunship/Legends|gunship]] was shot down, and Mapper's leg was badly injured in the ensuing crash.<ref name="The Pengalan Tradeoff" />

His wounds would have slowed the other troopers down, and Kithe, who was now in command of the unit, was told that Mapper might have to be put down. However, Kithe found that avenue of approach unacceptable, and instead rigged an ambush for a CIS patrol which had been dispatched to locate the downed clone troopers. The ambush turned into a success for the ARC's and the [[Squad/Legends|squad]] managed to gain control of an [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] that would allow them to carry Mapper with them to [[Tur Lorkin]], which was the nearest village. Mapper, who happened to have a working [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] with detailed maps of the area, was appointed navigator, thus giving him his nickname.<ref name="The Pengalan Tradeoff" />

Mapper had the best infiltration skills of the unit, but his splinted leg limited his usefulness, keeping him sidelined for much of the action in Tur Lorkin. Instead, while his fellow clones and Kithe attacked Tur Lorkin's hidden [[diamond boron missile]] factory, Mapper waited outside on a modified [[Single Trooper Aerial Platform/Legends|STAP]], where he was able to provide crucial support fire as the team exited the factory, destroying most of the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] that were laying in ambush.<ref name="The Pengalan Tradeoff" />

From there, the unit was able to flee the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] on a stolen [[Starship/Legends|starship]], confident that their mission was complete.<ref name="The Pengalan Tradeoff" />

===Tarhassan===
{{Quote|We're working for idiots. And you promised to do everything they said.|Mapper to Joram Kithe|<ref name="League of Spies" />}}
The injury that Mapper sustained on Pengalan IV required time to heal, and in the process, kept him away from the rest of his unit. During this time he was assigned to Kithe who had now accepted a full-time commission as a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] in [[Republic Intelligence/Legends|Republic Intelligence]]. Serving as his partner and bodyguard, Mapper took the alias ''Mapper Gann'', and grew a beard to pass himself off as a non-clone. Following his recovery, Mapper and Kithe were assigned a mission to the planet [[Tarhassan]] where Mapper was able to put his considerable skills to good use, and during which he and Kithe were tasked to a team of agents assigned with rescuing [[Edbit Teeks]].<ref name="League of Spies" />

Unfortunately, Kithe's success as an accountant-turned-intelligence agent had spawned a Republic program to duplicate his success. As a result Mapper and Kithe were assigned to three bureaucrats on their first intelligence mission. Kithe had to intervene several times to correct blunders from the other agents, and had to resort to going behind mission [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Cherek Tuhm]]'s back in order to save the life of a prisoner and get information on Teeks. Mapper served very well as backup to Kithe, and the other agents became rather fond of him, concentrating their ire on Kithe instead.<ref name="League of Spies" />

Nonetheless, Mapper considered them idiots, and wasn't above knocking Tuhm unconscious during their jailbreak of Teeks in order to keep the man from fouling up the operation. Ultimately, Kithe and Mapper were able to successfully complete their assigned mission with help from [[Tinian Hanther]], the most competent of the group they were attached to.<ref name="League of Spies" />

==Personality and traits==
Mapper was a talented soldier who was known to have been quite adept at the art of infiltration. He was also known to have always carried a datapad within his kit which he constantly kept up to date with maps concerning the planets of any mission he found himself on.<ref name="The Pengalan Tradeoff" />

==Behind the scenes==
Mapper was created by author [[Aaron Allston]] and illustrated by [[Tommy Lee Edwards]] for the short story [[The Pengalan Tradeoff|''The Clone Wars: The Pengalan Tradeoff'']] which was first published in [[Star Wars Insider 65|''Star Wars Insider'' 65]]<ref name="The Pengalan Tradeoff" /> on [[January 21]], [[2003]].<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=community/fanclub/mag/news20021219.html|text=''Insider'' #65 & ''Bantha Tracks'' #2}}</ref>
